name: Chinese Fire Pot
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Canned water chestnuts (drained and sliced)
8 oz Canned bamboo shoots (drained and sliced)
recipeInstructions:
1. It is not necessary to use all ingredients listed here as long as you offer an interesting blend of meats, fish, and vegetables. Other meats and vegetables can be substituted if desired.
2. Place beef, chicken, and fish in the freezer and chill until firm to touch but not frozen. Slice beef and chicken into strips 1/4-inch thick and about 2 inches long. Cut fish into 3/4-inch cubes. Shell and devein shrimp. Chop cabbage into bite-size chunks. Clean mushrooms. If using forest mushrooms, remove and discard stems. Slice mushrooms and sprinkle with lemon juice. Cut off and discard the root portion of enoki mushrooms. Separate clusters as much as possible. Wash, trim ends, and string pea pods. Clean green onions and cut in halves lengthwise, including the green portion. Cut into 2-inch lengths. Clean spinach and discard thick stems.
3. To serve, arrange beef, chicken, fish, shrimp, cabbage, forest mushrooms, enoki mushrooms, snow peas, green onions, spinach leaves, water chestnuts, and bamboo shoots in individual rows on large platters or serving plates.
4. Bring broth to boil. Place heating unit under Chinese hot pot and pour boiling broth into the hot-pot bowl. Using a Chinese wire ladle and chopsticks or fondue forks, each person places whatever ingredients are desired into the hot broth to poach. When cooked (this will take only a few moments), ingredients are then dipped into sweet-and-sour sauce, soy sauce, or hot mustard as desired, and eaten with noodles, adding cilantro if desired.
5. Note: The special pot needed can be purchased at Chinese shops.
